Prompt
U
Oracle Events
- Search for StormCloud-related emails using search_emails (query: "StormCloud" or "Liam Park" or "Jae-won Kim" or similar, across INBOX and SENT folders). The agent needs to discover: Liam Park's escalation email to Mina listing four issues before the leadership review (unsigned Austin lease, missing goodwill credit, stuck stipend reimbursement, unanswered referral); Mina's earlier recovery email to Tom Wexler promising a $500 goodwill credit on the next invoice after a February flight booking error; Carmen Reyes's April 8 email to Suki Patel flagging that Jae-won Kim's Barton Creek Flats lease is still pending the corporate guarantor packet and the landlord has not countersigned; Suki's April 9 reply promising to send the guarantor packet by end of week (which she never did); Jae-won Kim's April 15 support email asking about his lease documents; Omar Ibrahim's generic support reply without recognizing the active housing failure; Chloe Vance's ops review confirming the process failure; Julian Brooks's email contextualizing the $500 credit history and noting Marcus Thorne discovered it was posted to cancelled estimate EST-2026-0287 instead of active invoice INV-2026-0319; and Mina's reconstruction email to the team laying out the full chain of failures.
- Search for StormCloud-related emails about the stipend freeze and Simone Richter using search_emails (query: "Simone Richter" or "standing desk" or "stipend" or similar). The agent needs to discover: Simone Richter's email about her $340 standing desk reimbursement submitted March 25 still pending; Mina's forwarding of the issue to Marcus Thorne; and Marcus Thorne's reply stating that no stipend releases will be processed and no one-off exceptions made until the fraud review is complete (blanket freeze).
- Search for emails about the Ridgeline Robotics referral using search_emails (query: "Ridgeline" or "Anya Petrova" or "Kenji Watanabe" or similar). The agent needs to discover: Anya Petrova's April 18 warm introduction email to David Chen (CC'd to Mina and Kenji Watanabe) connecting MoveOps with Kenji Watanabe, HR Director at Ridgeline Robotics, for a batch of 5 Austin employee relocations over May–June; Kenji Watanabe's follow-up email on April 21 asking about services and timeline; and the fact that neither email received a response until April 24 when David created the CRM entries during the reconstruction.
- Search Slack messages using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "StormCloud" or "Jae-won" or "goodwill credit" or "Ridgeline" or similar). The agent needs to discover: internal discussions about the StormCloud account crisis; Marcus Thorne's confirmation in Slack that the $500 goodwill credit was posted to cancelled estimate EST-2026-0287 instead of the active invoice INV-2026-0319; David Chen's April 18–19 Slack celebration of the Ridgeline referral from Anya Petrova without anyone actually contacting Kenji; Chloe's ops audit findings about Suki's missed guarantor follow-up; and discussions about the stipend freeze policy.
- Search CRM for StormCloud company record, deals, and engagements using crm_search_companies (name: "StormCloud") and crm_search_deals (company_id: "company_stormcloud" or dealname: "StormCloud"). The agent needs to discover: the StormCloud Analytics company record (company_stormcloud); the original proposal deal (deal_stormcloud, $25,650, stage proposalMade); the closed-won deal (deal_stormcloud_won, $26,400, 3 premium relocations at $8,800 each); and any existing engagement notes. The agent should also use crm_list_engagements (company_ids: ["company_stormcloud"]) to review account history.
- Search CRM for Ridgeline Robotics using crm_search_companies (name: "Ridgeline") and crm_search_deals (dealname: "Ridgeline" or similar). The agent needs to discover whether the Ridgeline Robotics opportunity was ever entered into the CRM. In the universe data, a CRM company (crm_company_d07c0ec27cf2), lead (crm_lead_de04045463c3, referral from Anya Petrova), and deal (crm_deal_a95b4721982a, $8,750, Qualified To Buy, created April 24) were created during the reconstruction, the agent should verify this exists and confirm it is being actively pursued.
- Search Airtable relocation records using airtable_search_records (base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001", table_name: "Relocations", field_name: "Company", value: "StormCloud Analytics") or airtable_list_records. The agent needs to find: Jae-won Kim (recJaeWonKimStormCloudAustin, Seattle→Austin, Status: In Progress, Coordinator: Suki Patel, with notes indicating the record incorrectly showed Completed at one point while the lease was still pending guarantor paperwork); Jamie Reeves (recReloc00000017, Denver→Austin, Completed, Coordinator: Blessing Okafor, with the $180 hotel + $500 goodwill credit note); Sasha Petrov (recReloc00000018, Completed); and Colin Nakamura (recReloc00000019, Completed).
- Search Airtable for Suki Patel's other active moves using airtable_search_records (base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001", table_name: "Relocations", field_name: "Assigned Coordinator", value: "Suki Patel") or airtable_list_records filtered by coordinator. The agent needs to find that Suki has approximately 9 In Progress relocations across multiple clients: Daniel Reeves (Vectral, Seattle→San Diego), Maya Johansson (Vectral, Portland→San Diego), Andre Mitchell (Mosaic, Columbus→Boise), Simone Richter (BrightLoop, Chicago→Boston, rush), Marcus Webb (BrightLoop, Atlanta→Boston, vehicle shipping), Terrence Nakamura (Vectral, Austin→SF, ADA + split shipment), Camille Oduya (Vectral, Boston→Seattle, tight timeline), Deshawn Hartley (Mosaic, Tampa→Denver, hard April 19 deadline for Automate 2026 trade show), and Jae-won Kim (StormCloud). This demonstrates the overload that caused Suki to drop the StormCloud guarantor follow-up.
- Search Airtable Stipend Transactions for Simone Richter using airtable_search_records (base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001", table_name: "Stipend Transactions", field_name: "Employee Name", value: "Simone Richter" or similar). The agent will find no stipend transaction record for Simone Richter in Airtable; the $340 standing desk reimbursement claim is documented only through email (Simone's email from simone.richter@stormcloud.io to mina.hashimoto@moveops.com about the March 25 submission, Mina's forward to Marcus Thorne, and Marcus's reply confirming the blanket freeze) and Slack (C005 finance channel discussions). The absence of an Airtable record confirms the reimbursement was never formally processed into the stipend tracking system.
- Search Linear for StormCloud-related issues using linear_list_issues (query: "StormCloud" or "Jae-won" or similar). The agent needs to discover: issue "StormCloud Relocation – Jae-won Kim – SEA → AUS" (linear_issue_c97359216490, #284, P2, assigned to Suki Patel, prematurely closed on April 10 with a batch-closeout comment despite the open guarantor dependency); issue "Follow up with Jae-won Kim on requested Austin lease documents" (linear_issue_e7bcaf6978f0, #412, P4, unassigned, labeled Support/Low Priority/StormCloud, created from Omar's low-priority triage of Jae-won's April 15 support email); and issue "StormCloud account incident reconstruction, Jae-won housing, stipend hold, referral drop" (linear_issue_a49b4d199139, P1, assigned to Mina, due April 24). The agent should also read comments using linear_list_comments (issueId for the prematurely closed ticket) to find Suki's batch-closeout comment where she marked the relocation complete while acknowledging the UrbanNest guarantor docs were still pending.
- Search QuickBooks for StormCloud invoices and financial records using quickbooks_search_invoices (criteria referencing "StormCloud" or similar) and quickbooks_search_customers (criteria referencing "StormCloud"). The agent needs to find: invoice INV-2026-002 ($26,400 for 3 premium relocations, customer cust_stormcloud, billed to Tom Wexler). The agent should also investigate the $500 goodwill credit misapplication, emails and Slack reference it being posted to cancelled estimate EST-2026-0287 instead of active invoice INV-2026-0319, though these specific document numbers may only appear in the email/Slack narrative rather than as separate QuickBooks line items.
- Search calendar for upcoming StormCloud events using calendar_search_events (query: "StormCloud" or similar). The agent needs to check whether there are any scheduled meetings or events related to StormCloud that Mina should prepare for. The universe contains no StormCloud calendar events, so the agent should report finding none.
- Look up contact details for Liam Park, Carmen Reyes, and Chloe Vance using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Liam Park"), contacts_search_contacts (query: "Carmen Reyes"), and contacts_search_contacts (query: "Chloe Vance") to obtain email addresses for the write actions: liam.park@stormcloud.io, carmen.reyes@urbannestsolutions.com, chloe.vance@moveops.com.
- Send an email to Liam Park using send_email (sender: "mina.hashimoto@moveops.com", recipients: ["liam.park@stormcloud.io"]) with a full breakdown of each issue he raised and what MoveOps is doing to resolve each one. The email should cover: (1) Jae-won Kim's unsigned Austin lease at Barton Creek Flats, acknowledging that Suki Patel promised Carmen Reyes the corporate guarantor packet on April 9 but never sent it, that the relocation was incorrectly marked complete, and that the guarantor paperwork is being sent to UrbanNest immediately; (2) the $500 goodwill credit, acknowledging it was posted to cancelled estimate EST-2026-0287 instead of active invoice INV-2026-0319 and committing to correct it; (3) Simone Richter's $340 standing desk reimbursement stuck under a blanket stipend freeze with no exceptions during the ongoing fraud review; and (4) the Ridgeline Robotics referral from Anya Petrova, acknowledging the delayed response and confirming MoveOps has now reached out to Kenji Watanabe.
- Send an email to Carmen Reyes at UrbanNest using send_email (sender: "mina.hashimoto@moveops.com", recipients: ["carmen.reyes@urbannestsolutions.com"]) regarding Jae-won Kim's lease at Barton Creek Flats and the corporate guarantor packet that needs to be sent, including the guarantor letter, EIN confirmation, and certificate of insurance that Suki previously committed to providing.
- Create a CRM engagement for StormCloud using crm_create_engagement (engagement_type: "note", body: account incident log, company_ids: ["company_stormcloud"]) documenting the full chain of failures discovered during reconstruction: Jae-won's unsigned lease due to dropped guarantor follow-up, the $500 credit misapplied to the wrong estimate, Simone Richter's frozen stipend, the 6-day gap on the Ridgeline referral, and Omar's misprioritization driven by false Airtable/Linear completion status.
- Post a rundown in #operations for Chloe using conversations_add_message (channel_id: "C006", payload: StormCloud operational status summary). The message should cover: the current state of Jae-won Kim's housing (guarantor packet being sent, need to confirm landlord countersignature and extend temp housing if needed), Suki Patel's overload across 9 active relocations that caused the StormCloud drop, the prematurely closed Linear ticket and false Airtable completion status that cascaded into Omar's misprioritization, and any ops-level actions needed to prevent recurrence.
- Update Linear issues to fix records showing wrong status using linear_update_issue (id: "linear_issue_c97359216490") to reopen the prematurely closed Jae-won Kim relocation ticket, or linear_create_comment (issueId: for the reconstruction issue) to document the current resolution status. The prompt asks "whether our records actually reflect reality or if anything got marked done that shouldn't have been," which strongly implies the agent should fix the prematurely closed Linear ticket once discovered. While not explicitly listed as an action item, reopening the ticket is a natural implicit requirement flowing from the investigation findings.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.
universe_idmcp-advanced-3-20-moveops-metadata

🔍 Oracle Events
Oracle Events
- Search for StormCloud-related emails using search_emails (query: "StormCloud" or "Liam Park" or "Jae-won Kim" or similar, across INBOX and SENT folders). The agent needs to discover: Liam Park's escalation email to Mina listing four issues before the leadership review (unsigned Austin lease, missing goodwill credit, stuck stipend reimbursement, unanswered referral); Mina's earlier recovery email to Tom Wexler promising a $500 goodwill credit on the next invoice after a February flight booking error; Carmen Reyes's April 8 email to Suki Patel flagging that Jae-won Kim's Barton Creek Flats lease is still pending the corporate guarantor packet and the landlord has not countersigned; Suki's April 9 reply promising to send the guarantor packet by end of week (which she never did); Jae-won Kim's April 15 support email asking about his lease documents; Omar Ibrahim's generic support reply without recognizing the active housing failure; Chloe Vance's ops review confirming the process failure; Julian Brooks's email contextualizing the $500 credit history and noting Marcus Thorne discovered it was posted to cancelled estimate EST-2026-0287 instead of active invoice INV-2026-0319; and Mina's reconstruction email to the team laying out the full chain of failures.
- Search for StormCloud-related emails about the stipend freeze and Simone Richter using search_emails (query: "Simone Richter" or "standing desk" or "stipend" or similar). The agent needs to discover: Simone Richter's email about her $340 standing desk reimbursement submitted March 25 still pending; Mina's forwarding of the issue to Marcus Thorne; and Marcus Thorne's reply stating that no stipend releases will be processed and no one-off exceptions made until the fraud review is complete (blanket freeze).
- Search for emails about the Ridgeline Robotics referral using search_emails (query: "Ridgeline" or "Anya Petrova" or "Kenji Watanabe" or similar). The agent needs to discover: Anya Petrova's April 18 warm introduction email to David Chen (CC'd to Mina and Kenji Watanabe) connecting MoveOps with Kenji Watanabe, HR Director at Ridgeline Robotics, for a batch of 5 Austin employee relocations over May–June; Kenji Watanabe's follow-up email on April 21 asking about services and timeline; and the fact that neither email received a response until April 24 when David created the CRM entries during the reconstruction.
- Search Slack messages using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "StormCloud" or "Jae-won" or "goodwill credit" or "Ridgeline" or similar). The agent needs to discover: internal discussions about the StormCloud account crisis; Marcus Thorne's confirmation in Slack that the $500 goodwill credit was posted to cancelled estimate EST-2026-0287 instead of the active invoice INV-2026-0319; David Chen's April 18–19 Slack celebration of the Ridgeline referral from Anya Petrova without anyone actually contacting Kenji; Chloe's ops audit findings about Suki's missed guarantor follow-up; and discussions about the stipend freeze policy.
- Search CRM for StormCloud company record, deals, and engagements using crm_search_companies (name: "StormCloud") and crm_search_deals (company_id: "company_stormcloud" or dealname: "StormCloud"). The agent needs to discover: the StormCloud Analytics company record (company_stormcloud); the original proposal deal (deal_stormcloud, $25,650, stage proposalMade); the closed-won deal (deal_stormcloud_won, $26,400, 3 premium relocations at $8,800 each); and any existing engagement notes. The agent should also use crm_list_engagements (company_ids: ["company_stormcloud"]) to review account history.
- Search CRM for Ridgeline Robotics using crm_search_companies (name: "Ridgeline") and crm_search_deals (dealname: "Ridgeline" or similar). The agent needs to discover whether the Ridgeline Robotics opportunity was ever entered into the CRM. In the universe data, a CRM company (crm_company_d07c0ec27cf2), lead (crm_lead_de04045463c3, referral from Anya Petrova), and deal (crm_deal_a95b4721982a, $8,750, Qualified To Buy, created April 24) were created during the reconstruction, the agent should verify this exists and confirm it is being actively pursued.
- Search Airtable relocation records using airtable_search_records (base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001", table_name: "Relocations", field_name: "Company", value: "StormCloud Analytics") or airtable_list_records. The agent needs to find: Jae-won Kim (recJaeWonKimStormCloudAustin, Seattle→Austin, Status: In Progress, Coordinator: Suki Patel, with notes indicating the record incorrectly showed Completed at one point while the lease was still pending guarantor paperwork); Jamie Reeves (recReloc00000017, Denver→Austin, Completed, Coordinator: Blessing Okafor, with the $180 hotel + $500 goodwill credit note); Sasha Petrov (recReloc00000018, Completed); and Colin Nakamura (recReloc00000019, Completed).
- Search Airtable for Suki Patel's other active moves using airtable_search_records (base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001", table_name: "Relocations", field_name: "Assigned Coordinator", value: "Suki Patel") or airtable_list_records filtered by coordinator. The agent needs to find that Suki has approximately 9 In Progress relocations across multiple clients: Daniel Reeves (Vectral, Seattle→San Diego), Maya Johansson (Vectral, Portland→San Diego), Andre Mitchell (Mosaic, Columbus→Boise), Simone Richter (BrightLoop, Chicago→Boston, rush), Marcus Webb (BrightLoop, Atlanta→Boston, vehicle shipping), Terrence Nakamura (Vectral, Austin→SF, ADA + split shipment), Camille Oduya (Vectral, Boston→Seattle, tight timeline), Deshawn Hartley (Mosaic, Tampa→Denver, hard April 19 deadline for Automate 2026 trade show), and Jae-won Kim (StormCloud). This demonstrates the overload that caused Suki to drop the StormCloud guarantor follow-up.
- Search Airtable Stipend Transactions for Simone Richter using airtable_search_records (base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001", table_name: "Stipend Transactions", field_name: "Employee Name", value: "Simone Richter" or similar). The agent will find no stipend transaction record for Simone Richter in Airtable; the $340 standing desk reimbursement claim is documented only through email (Simone's email from simone.richter@stormcloud.io to mina.hashimoto@moveops.com about the March 25 submission, Mina's forward to Marcus Thorne, and Marcus's reply confirming the blanket freeze) and Slack (C005 finance channel discussions). The absence of an Airtable record confirms the reimbursement was never formally processed into the stipend tracking system.
- Search Linear for StormCloud-related issues using linear_list_issues (query: "StormCloud" or "Jae-won" or similar). The agent needs to discover: issue "StormCloud Relocation – Jae-won Kim – SEA → AUS" (linear_issue_c97359216490, #284, P2, assigned to Suki Patel, prematurely closed on April 10 with a batch-closeout comment despite the open guarantor dependency); issue "Follow up with Jae-won Kim on requested Austin lease documents" (linear_issue_e7bcaf6978f0, #412, P4, unassigned, labeled Support/Low Priority/StormCloud, created from Omar's low-priority triage of Jae-won's April 15 support email); and issue "StormCloud account incident reconstruction, Jae-won housing, stipend hold, referral drop" (linear_issue_a49b4d199139, P1, assigned to Mina, due April 24). - Search QuickBooks for StormCloud invoices and financial records using quickbooks_search_invoices (criteria referencing "StormCloud" or similar) and quickbooks_search_customers (criteria referencing "StormCloud"). The agent needs to find: invoice INV-2026-002 ($26,400 for 3 premium relocations, customer cust_stormcloud, billed to Tom Wexler). The agent should also investigate the $500 goodwill credit misapplication, emails and Slack reference it being posted to cancelled estimate EST-2026-0287 instead of active invoice INV-2026-0319, though these specific document numbers may only appear in the email/Slack narrative rather than as separate QuickBooks line items.
- Search calendar for upcoming StormCloud events using calendar_search_events (query: "StormCloud" or similar). The agent needs to check whether there are any scheduled meetings or events related to StormCloud that Mina should prepare for. The universe contains no StormCloud calendar events, so the agent should report finding none.
- Look up contact details for Liam Park, Carmen Reyes, and Chloe Vance using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Liam Park"), contacts_search_contacts (query: "Carmen Reyes"), and contacts_search_contacts (query: "Chloe Vance") to obtain email addresses for the write actions: liam.park@stormcloud.io, carmen.reyes@urbannestsolutions.com, chloe.vance@moveops.com.
- Send an email to Liam Park using send_email (sender: "mina.hashimoto@moveops.com", recipients: ["liam.park@stormcloud.io"]) with a full breakdown of each issue he raised and what MoveOps is doing to resolve each one. The email should cover: (1) Jae-won Kim's unsigned Austin lease at Barton Creek Flats, acknowledging that Suki Patel promised Carmen Reyes the corporate guarantor packet on April 9 but never sent it, that the relocation was incorrectly marked complete, and that the guarantor paperwork is being sent to UrbanNest immediately; (2) the $500 goodwill credit, acknowledging it was posted to cancelled estimate EST-2026-0287 instead of active invoice INV-2026-0319 and committing to correct it; (3) Simone Richter's $340 standing desk reimbursement stuck under a blanket stipend freeze with no exceptions during the ongoing fraud review; and (4) the Ridgeline Robotics referral from Anya Petrova, acknowledging the delayed response and confirming MoveOps has now reached out to Kenji Watanabe.
- Send an email to Carmen Reyes at UrbanNest using send_email (sender: "mina.hashimoto@moveops.com", recipients: ["carmen.reyes@urbannestsolutions.com"]) regarding Jae-won Kim's lease at Barton Creek Flats and the corporate guarantor packet that needs to be sent, including the guarantor letter, EIN confirmation, and certificate of insurance that Suki previously committed to providing.
- Create a CRM engagement for StormCloud using crm_create_engagement (engagement_type: "note", body: account incident log, company_ids: ["company_stormcloud"]) documenting the full chain of failures discovered during reconstruction: Jae-won's unsigned lease due to dropped guarantor follow-up, the $500 credit misapplied to the wrong estimate, Simone Richter's frozen stipend, the 6-day gap on the Ridgeline referral, and Omar's misprioritization driven by false Airtable/Linear completion status.
- Post a rundown in #operations for Chloe using conversations_add_message (channel_id: "C006", payload: StormCloud operational status summary). The message should cover: the current state of Jae-won Kim's housing (guarantor packet being sent, need to confirm landlord countersignature and extend temp housing if needed), Suki Patel's overload across 9 active relocations that caused the StormCloud drop, the prematurely closed Linear ticket and false Airtable completion status that cascaded into Omar's misprioritization, and any ops-level actions needed to prevent recurrence.
- Update Linear issues to fix records showing wrong status using linear_update_issue (id: "linear_issue_c97359216490") to reopen the prematurely closed Jae-won Kim relocation ticket, or linear_create_comment (issueId: for the reconstruction issue) to document the current resolution status. The prompt asks "whether our records actually reflect reality or if anything got marked done that shouldn't have been," which strongly implies the agent should fix the prematurely closed Linear ticket once discovered. While not explicitly listed as an action item, reopening the ticket is a natural implicit requirement flowing from the investigation findings.
